title: MacOS: When a connection has an SSH identity file, podman-remote always ignores ssh-agent
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When a `podman-remote` connection has an identity file associated with it, it will always ask for the private key password for that identity, even if the key is loaded in the SSH agent.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On macOS, add a connection with an SSH identity file that has a password.

2. Add that SSH identity to the SSH agent using `ssh-add`.

3. Run `podman info` and see that it asks for the private key password.

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman` always asks for the SSH key's password.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected it to behave like `ssh`: When running `ssh -i`, the SSH agent is queried first, and if the desired key is present there then it is used.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.17
Built:        Mon Aug 30 12:15:26 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Mon Aug  2 12:39:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: The existing code is working as expected. We prompt for the password to decode the identity file using the golang ssh library. If you have the same key in your ssh-agent, we default to that copy. We would need to research the golang ssh library bindings to see if we can get the sha256 fingerprint from an identity file without the passphrase.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@jwhonce is this a ssh verssus go-ssh problem?

If I'm reading the code properly, I think [this `sshClient` function](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2df9bdc00948e24855f3da42e31a6d273861e380/pkg/bindings/connection.go#L187) first makes an ordered list of client identities to present to the SSH server, starting with the passed-in identity and then adding SSH agent identities.

Then it attempts to dedup the list based on sha256 fingerprint.
There's a comment seems to indicate that agent keys will be preferred over an identity file at this step ("`Dedup signers based on fingerprint, ssh-agent keys override CONTAINER_SSHKEY`").

My hunch is, either there's a bug in that deduping code, or the private key password prompt is presented before `sshClient` is even run.

@umohnani8 @cdoern was going to look into this, but I think his suggested change would ignore the agent completely if the identity file was given.

/cc @baude
